name: Georgia Peaches-And-Cream Cake
recipeInstructions:
- Preheat the oven to 375°F. Allow it to fully heat up for 15 minutes before baking.
- Place the rack in the center of the oven.
- Butter an 8-inch square glass baking pan.
- Cut the peaches into 1/2-inch slices and combine them with sugar, lemon juice, tapioca, and nutmeg. Gently cook over high heat until syrup begins to bubble for about 4 minutes. Set aside.
- Combine flour, sugar, butter, and cinnamon in a food processor or pastry blender until the mixture resembles small peas. Set aside.
- In a separate bowl, stir sour cream and baking soda together and set aside.
- In another bowl, beat egg, sugar, and butter with a mixer or processor until light and fluffy. Add vanilla extract and mix well. Gradually fold in flour, baking powder, and salt, mixing gently after each addition.
- Pour the warm peach mixture into the bottom of the prepared pan. Spread the batter evenly over the peaches using a rubber spatula. It's acceptable if the peaches are not completely covered.
- Sprinkle the crumb topping over the batter.
- Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake (not the fruit) comes out clean, about 25-to-28 minutes.
